What the interleave buys (read off the grid):
· inverse dynamics — noisy ~a of chunk c attends clean z of chunks ≤ c
(its own observations) but never future video;
· forward dynamics — noisy ~z of chunk c+1 attends clean a of chunk c
· noisy video and noisy action never attend each other (fids differ by
construction) — denoising targets stay independent given clean
· within a chunk, noisy tokens are bidirectional (parallel generation),
across chunks strictly causal — the paper's “causality without giving up
the same 16 tokens as a sequence (hatched / ~ = noisy, being denoised):
sit strictly between video chunks c and c+1. Example: F=4 frames, chunk K=2, 1 token per frame. wan_va/modules/model.py:93-201
Sequence layout [noisy z | clean z | noisy a | clean a], frame ids interleaved as fid_z = 2·(f//K), fid_a = 2·(f//K)+1 — actions of chunk c
